mappings.rationale: Covers literary developments from the Heian period through Tokugawa, directly relevant to subsections B3, C1d, C2c, C3b, and C4c.
Directly covers the samurai class, bushido, daimyo, and shogunate system central to subsections C1-C4 on feudal Japan.
Covers the Meiji Restoration referenced in the section title and provides context for Tokugawa Japan's transition to modernity in subsection C4.
Relevant to subsections C1d and C4d on Neo-Confucianism's influence during the Kamakura and Tokugawa periods.
Relevant to subsections B2a-b and C1d on the introduction and flowering of Buddhism in Japan, including Zen Buddhism.
Directly covers subsection D on Korean history from the Three Kingdoms through the Yi dynasty.
Relevant to subsection C1c on the Mongol invasions of Japan in 1274 and 1281 and subsection D4 on Mongol invasions of Korea.
Relevant to the successive dynasties and shogunates from the Yamato court through the Tokugawa that structure Japan's political history.
Relevant to subsection B1 on Japan's prehistoric Jomon and Yayoi cultures and their archaeological evidence.
